mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales captures underlying consumer demand strength; persistent positive momentum in this series translates directly into corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS momentum coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 45% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy sharply elevates the equity discount rate and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not statistically below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in CPIAUCSL and UNRATE creates a stagflationary environment where cost pressures rise while labor income growth slows; this joint deterioration drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and UNRATE produces a positive rank correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window, or the combined signal underperforms the standalone UNRATE signal by more than 50% in hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production predict positive S&P 500 returns specifically during post-recession recovery when capacity restoration signals earnings rebound; outside this regime the relationship weakens.
failure_condition: During declared post-recession recovery periods, month-over-month INDPRO increases fail to coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in at least 60% of cases, or the information coefficient drops below 0.02 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate over a three-month window signals tightening labor markets and wage-driven consumption strength, which translates into positive S&P 500 earnings revisions and forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Three-month declines in UNRATE are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,200, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered months, or the median return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales momentum combined with low federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand without restrictive monetary policy, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of high RSAFS and low FEDFUNDS fails to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in quarterly real GDP growth signals broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 accelerations are followed by negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rank correlation between GDPC1 changes and returns is negative over any 5-year window.
